event: non-contrast head CT revealed hemorrhage in third and fourth ventricles
hemorrhage in bilateral lateral ventricles with left dominance
source of bleeding unclear in arterial phase
spot enhancement on lateral wall of anterior horn of left lateral ventricle in venous phase
blood pressure control initiated in intensive care unit
cerebral angiogram on third day of admission
aneurysm (3.8 mm) at distal site of mLSA from proximal A1 segment of left ACA
guiding sheath navigated into petrous portion of left internal carotid artery
Marathon catheter advanced to left A1
mLSA arising in direction reverse to path of left A1
compression study of right common carotid artery confirmed AComA
guiding sheath introduced into right internal carotid artery
Marathon advanced through AComA into mLSA origin
unable to advance to site near aneurysm
changed to DeFrictor nano catheter and TENROU S10 guide wire
selective angiography confirmed catheter tip proximal to aneurysm
perforating artery distal and proximal to aneurysm filled with NBCA
no hemorrhagic complications on postoperative CT
no cerebral infarction on postoperative CT
